clue: Row about newspaper backing Panama?
blog: definition: TITFER is rhyming slang: ‘titfer tat’ => ‘hat’; and a Panama is a type of hat TIER=”Row”, around FT (Financial Times, “newspaper”) reversed/”backing”

clue: Silly positions must be rejected – I would
blog: PUTS=”positions” (as a verb), reversed/”rejected”; plus I’D=”I would”

clue: Exploit flyer with imperfect tenses to get personal benefit
Bowler may be here to give evidence
blog: a cricket bowler may be AT a TEST match

clue: Proprietor with joiner in funny comeback
blog: AND=joining word=”joiner”, inside DROLL=”funny” reversed/coming back

clue: Nightcap on vacation during jovial preparatory celebration
blog: N-[ightca]-P, vacated of its inner letters; inside HEARTY=”jovial” edit to correct typo in “HEN PARTY” – thanks to AlanC in the comments

clue: John is one outwardly for hire
blog: I=”one”, with TO LET=”for hire” on the outside

clue: First couple of letters dispatched, so not here
blog: AB=”First couple of letters [in the alphabet]”; plus SENT=”dispatched”

clue: Sounds like Bath perhaps being in short supply
blog: sounds like ‘spa city’, such as “Bath perhaps”

clue: Settle where stated in Oxford or Derby
blog: definition: Oxford and Derby are types of shoes to FOOT or “Settle” a bill; plus WEAR which sounds (“stated”) like ‘where’
def_text: Settle where stated in Oxford or Derby

clue: Learnt to play the horn
blog: anagram/”to play” of (Learnt)*

clue: Best in everything for big Brits
blog: definition: an idiom describing the best part among what is available big Brits might be described as ‘the fat of the land/Britain’

clue: Stick poster, but not over there!
blog: AD (advertisement, “poster”) + HERE (as opposed to “over there”)

clue: From Goethe, a tremendous play enacted here
blog: hidden in [Goe]-THE A TRE-[mendous]

clue: I then took odds on twenty in order to get hitched
blog: anagram/’in order” of (I then took t e t )*, with t e t coming from the “odds”/odd letters of t w e n t y

clue: One chooses shock treatment in role reversal
blog: ECT (ElectroConvulsive Therapy, “shock treatment”); inside reversal of ROLE

clue: See 2
Want to start eating potato
blog: definition: a variety of potato DESIRE=”Want” + start of E-[ating]

clue: Disparate motel with a twist in the tail? End of story!
blog: MOT- EL with a switch in order (“twist”) of the two “tail” letters; plus end of [stor]-Y

clue: Fill up ten forms, that’s more than enough
blog: anagram/’formed from’: (Fill up ten)*

clue: Relative souper?
blog: BROTH=’soup’, so perhaps BROTH-ER=soup-er

clue: Prepared letters of support
blog: anagram/”Prepared” of (letters)*

clue: First and last to arrive can generate conflict
blog: anagram/’generated from’: (First e )*, with the e from the last letter of [arriv]- e

clue: Embarrassed to display university degree in an outhouse
blog: BA (Bachelor of Arts, “university degree”) in A SHED=”an outhouse”

clue: Finally enrol in crash diet mañana, got that, but not tomorrow
blog: definition: mañana has a tilde symbol (and the ‘tomorrow’ does not) final letter of [enro]-L in anagram/”crash” of (diet)*
